description_long: This bungalow features a sprawling, single-story layout with a complex, multi-sectioned dark tiled hip roof. The exterior walls are primarily light-colored, likely stucco or painted concrete, with dark wooden trim and support beams visible along the eaves and porch areas. A prominent covered porch runs along one side, supported by wooden posts and partially enclosed by railings, providing a sheltered outdoor space. The windows are modest in size and arranged asymmetrically, with some grouped together and others spaced apart, contributing to a casual, lived-in appearance. The structure sits slightly elevated above the ground, with steps leading up to the porch and main entrances. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional and practical elements, suitable for suburban or residential neighborhood settings.
description: A single-story bungalow with a dark hip roof, light walls, and a prominent covered porch supported by wooden posts. The windows are asymmetrically arranged, and the structure features a blend of traditional and practical design elements.
description_view_0: This view highlights the L-shaped layout, the expansive hip roof, and the covered porch area with visible support posts and railings.
description_view_1: From this angle, the main entrance and the depth of the porch are visible, along with the light-colored facade and the roof's overhang.
description_view_2: This side view emphasizes the long, continuous porch, the grouped windows, and the extended roofline covering the outdoor area.
description_view_3: This perspective shows the rear and side of the bungalow, with additional porch details, steps, and a clear view of the roof's complex geometry.
